title: ENCODE SCREEN cCRE Regulatory Annotation Resources

data_urls.access_notes: Use the portal and ENCODE file metadata for current cCRE releases.

data_urls.description: ENCODE file example used in prior SPECTRA resource acquisition
data_urls.access_notes: Verify accession, release, assembly, and cCRE version before reuse.

instructions: Use ENCODE portal metadata to select the intended cCRE release and genome assembly.
Record ENCODE accessions, cCRE version, assembly, interval definitions, and any resizing/windowing.
If joining to sequences, retrieve genome reference through a documented source and preserve coordinate transforms.

recommended_axes.name: regulatory_element_class
recommended_axes.definition: Shared or different cCRE class such as enhancer-like or promoter-like category.

recommended_axes.name: local_regulatory_context
recommended_axes.definition: Density or neighborhood of cCRE annotations around a sequence window.

recommended_axes.name: composition_control
recommended_axes.definition: GC/CpG/length/k-mer similarity used as a control for regulatory-class claims.

useful_for: Constructing coordinate-backed regulatory sequence hypothesis-test datasets.
Translating DNA model embedding signals into regulatory annotation hypotheses.
Testing local cCRE support and composition-regime explanations.
leakage_risks: Overlapping windows and duplicate cCRE IDs can leak across splits.
cCRE class can be confounded with GC/CpG/length composition.
Genome assembly and coordinate mismatches can create invalid joins.
